text: ‘Tumult of the mind’; the word in the Hebrew text means either ‘dismay’ or ‘ruin’; neither seems a very good parallel for the word ‘oppression’, and some have suspected corruption in the text.

The Latin version, probably through misunderstanding a word that is three times repeated, differs widely here from the Hebrew text, whose meaning seems to be ‘Let men strive against thee as they will, it is with no sanction of mine; whoever strives against thee shall fall because of thee (or perhaps, shall be compelled to fall in with thee)’.
